headlines.title: Energy bills to rise for millions as impact of Iran war hits
headlines.description: A household using a typical amount of energy will pay £221 a year more, under the regulator's new price cap.

headlines.title: Labour has 'no coherent plan' for country, says Blair
headlines.description: The ex-Labour PM says Sir Keir Starmer's government is in the "wrong position" ahead of the next election.

headlines.title: Six people die in water-related incidents during heatwave
headlines.description: At least five youngsters and a man in his 60s died, with a teenage boy reported missing in Cheshire.

headlines.title: Sturgeon defends 'no comment' police interview after SNP funds probe arrest
headlines.description: The former SNP leader's solicitor says her response was "standard" and she later provided written answers to detectives' questions.

headlines.title: Nasa unveils next steps to build permanent Moon base
headlines.description: Nasa plans to send hopping drones and roving vehicles to the Moon as part of plans for a permanent Moon base.

headlines.title: 'We knew somebody would die': Teenage patients 'ignored' before fatal NHS trust failures
headlines.description: Mental health patients say nobody listened to their concerns about a north-east England trust.

headlines.title: Ebola-hit DR Congo faces 'catastrophic collision' of disease and conflict, WHO warns
headlines.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said fighting in DR Congo was hampering efforts to stop spread

headlines.title: 'Shocking' rise in school suspensions for racist, ableist and homophobic abuse
headlines.description: Education experts and charities are calling for a national anti-bullying strategy in schools.

headlines.title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
headlines.description: At least three people were killed in the attack, which came despite a ceasefire with Hamas being in place.

headlines.title: Hundreds of dead sharks found washed-up on Welsh beaches
headlines.description: The dead fish, at Saundersfoot and Pembrey beaches, were probably part of a discarded catch.

headlines.title: Trump-backed challenger beats veteran US senator in Texas primary
headlines.description: The result sets up what will be a closely watched contest in November with Paxton's Democratic opponent.

headlines.title: Student loans inquiry finds many did not understand terms
headlines.description: The Treasury inquiry is looking at student loan plans in England and whether repayment terms are "reasonable".

headlines.title: The footballer setting the record straight after 46 years
headlines.description: Roly Gregoire, Sunderland's first black player, sometimes wishes he had never played football because of the racist abuse he found too painful to talk about for decades.

headlines.title: Can EU find a Russia whisperer to mediate an end to war in Ukraine?
headlines.description: With the US pulling out of trilateral talks with Russia and Ukraine, the EU is looking for potential candidates to step in.

headlines.title: How I won fantasy football in my first ever season - without using AI
headlines.description: Danish medical student Erik Ibsen, 23, says it's "insane" he beat more than 11 million FPL players.

headlines.title: Watch: Trump is transforming Washington - by adding his face everywhere
headlines.description: Sarah Smith visits places across the US capital where the president has hung banners, renamed a site or is rebuilding something.

headlines.title: How long will the heatwave last?
headlines.description: Following the hottest May day on record, Simon King looks at how many more days of heat you can expect.

headlines.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
headlines.description: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.

headlines.title: Bad Bunny joins Toy Story 5 cast as voice of forgotten pizza toy
headlines.description: The Puerto Rican star will make a cameo in the latest in the animated Disney film franchise.

headlines.title: Russia 'relentlessly targeting' critical infrastructure and democracy, GCHQ says
headlines.description: The spy agency's head will set out threats facing the UK and the measures she believes need to be taken to confront them on Wednesday.

headlines.title: Man charged over fatal shooting of award-winning farmer
headlines.description: The body of Charles Kinston, 23, was found in a field in Derbyshire on 29 January 2024.

headlines.title: Dozens killed in Lebanon as Israel intensifies strikes
headlines.description: Israel says it struck 100 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and fighters in Lebanon, after PM Benjamin Netanyahu vows to "crush" the Iran-backed group.

headlines.title: Around 100 firefighters tackle Golders Green blaze
headlines.description: The fire broke out around 06:45 BST at a supermarket below a block of flats in north-west London.

headlines.title: Hotel that refused to give tourist tap water acted lawfully, Italian court rules
headlines.description: The Italian Supreme Court rejected a tourist's claim that her consumer rights were breached when she was only offered €7 bottled mineral water.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Why Did Teenage Rapists Avoid Custody?
headlines.description: Sentences of boys spared custody over rape referred to Court of Appeal.

headlines.title: The political fight for American men
headlines.description: An interview with author and professor, Scott Galloway

headlines.title: Nuno to stay as West Ham boss after relegation
headlines.description: Nuno Espirito Santo will stay on as West Ham manager to lead their fight to get back to the Premier League at the first attempt, the club says.

headlines.title: Spurs needed 'complete reset', says under fire CEO
headlines.description: In a wide-ranging exclusive interview with BBC Sport, Tottenham chief executive Vinai Venkatesham talks about a number of issues the club needs to address.

headlines.title: The footballer setting record straight after 46 years
headlines.description: Roly Gregoire, Sunderland's first black player, sometimes wishes he had never played football because of the racist abuse he found too painful to talk about for 46 years.

headlines.title: 60 Palace 'troublemakers' ordered to leave Leipzig
headlines.description: Police say 60 Crystal Palace fans classified as "known troublemakers" have been ordered to leave Leipzig while two arrests were made before the Europa Conference League final.

headlines.title: Yapp makes history as first Lions Women coach
headlines.description: The ex-England scrum-half says she wants to 'create something special' on next year's inaugural tour in New Zealand.

headlines.title: Travelling solo and sharing rooms – what is life like on tennis’ lower rung?
headlines.description: Sleeping in a transit van is one of the money-saving tricks used by those on the lower rung of the tennis circuit.
